vocab.meaning: một yếu tố/sự kiện thay đổi hoàn toàn cục diện
vocab.example: The introduction of the new striker was a real game-changer; he turned the match around in minutes.

vocab.meaning: đến tận phút cuối cùng mới biết kết quả (gay cấn)
vocab.example: I love team sports because they are often down to the wire, keeping me in a state of suspense.

vocab.word: keep one's eye on the ball
vocab.meaning: tập trung cao độ vào mục tiêu
vocab.example: It's inspiring to see how professional athletes keep their eye on the ball despite the immense pressure from the crowd.

vocab.meaning: thắng một cách dễ dàng/áp đảo
vocab.example: Last night, the home team was in top form and they won the match hands down.

vocab.meaning: đánh bại đối thủ một cách thuyết phục/dễ dàng
vocab.example: I enjoy watching international tournaments where a dominant team can just blow the competition away with their skill.

vocab.meaning: một sân chơi bình đẳng/công bằng
vocab.example: Team games are great when there is a level playing field, making the outcome truly unpredictable.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực hơn để cải thiện phong độ/trình độ
vocab.example: When playing against a rival, every member of the squad has to step up their game to secure a win.

vocab.word: the ball is in someone's court
vocab.meaning: quyết định/trách nhiệm thuộc về ai đó
vocab.example: With five minutes left, the ball is in the captain's court to lead the team to a comeback.

vocab.word: get into the swing of things
vocab.meaning: bắt đầu quen với nhịp độ/vào guồng
vocab.example: It takes a few minutes for the players to get into the swing of things after the opening whistle.
Even if they lose, I respect a team that goes out there and gives it their best shot until the very end.
